text: Debugging is a key skill for programmers, and using a systematic approach makes it much easier. The first step is identifying bugs by carefully observing how your code behaves differently than expected. To find the cause, you can use debugging tools like breakpoints in your code editor, which pause the program so you can inspect variables. Another helpful strategy is logging, where you print messages to the console at various points in your code. This gives you a timeline of what’s happening. By following a structured process—reproducing the bug, checking one possible cause at a time, and testing your fix—you can resolve errors efficiently without guessing.
